question.text: Under Indian tax law, carry forward of speculative losses is allowed for:

answer.text: 4 assessment years from the year of loss

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Speculative losses can be carried forward for 4 assessment years (shorter than the 8-year period for normal business losses). **The Trap:** 8 years is for non-speculative business losses. Not indefinite. Carry forward IS allowed. **Cross-Exam Context:** Loss carry forward rules apply across all income types. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The 4-year limitation means speculative losses expire faster — if you can legitimately classify trading as hedging, the 8-year carry forward for business losses applies instead.
